guideline_references: Cancer Council Australia / Australian Cancer Network: Clinical Practice Guide for the diagnosis and management of Keratinocyte Cancer 2019
intentional_test_features: Over-triage resistance vs don't-miss-malignancy balance: an alarming acute presentation (sudden pain + bleeding + 'burning hot', high cancer anxiety) on an anticoagulated frail older adult is the red herring for acuity; the genuine scored competency is recognising a CHANGED lesion on a field-cancerised scalp that cannot be examined or biopsied remotely, arranging prompt in-person review to exclude SCC, and NOT over-escalating controllable minor bleeding to ED/000.

Telehealth reprojection: source is an in-person clinician who achieved haemostasis with direct pressure and a dressing and palpated the lesion base / regional nodes. The AI Doctor cannot examine, palpate, or biopsy — it recognises, instructs patient-deliverable haemostasis (firm direct pressure + dressing), and refers.
Anticoagulation interaction trap: patient on apixaban — NSAID/aspirin for pain must NOT be recommended (additive bleeding risk); this is the scoreable pharmacology judgment.
False-reassurance trap: telehealth cannot call this benign — a video cannot exclude squamous cell carcinoma; reassure-and-discharge with no referral is the critical under-triage.
Age (~78) and male sex inferred from 'elderly', 'physically frail', bald scalp and atrial fibrillation on apixaban — flagged for clinician confirmation.
Source category: DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue), source note DST-003.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
